title: The weirdness of neutrinos could completely rewrite particle physics
description: A philosopher has put forward an argument for rethinking how particles are defined within the standard model of particle physics
author: Karmela Padavic-Callaghan

title: A type of fibre that stimulates GLP-1 release approved for use in food
description: A form of fibre that boosts the release of the hunger-reducing hormone GLP-1 has been approved as safe by a European regulator, and could be added to foods within a year
author: Michael Le Page

title: Slowdown of AMOC ocean current may be gradual and reversible
description: Scientists worry that a surge of meltwater from Greenland could irreversibly collapse the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation, but new modelling suggests the weakening of the current could be reversed if CO2 levels come back down
author: Alec Luhn

title: First new open-ocean subsea research habitat in 40 years launched
description: The ocean floor is a vast source of potentially revolutionary scientific discovery – yet it remains largely inaccessible. For many marine scientists, they could only dream of living and working underwater. That changes now.
author: New Scientist

title: The world's fastest spider tops 3.5 metres per second
description: The most comprehensive database ever compiled of how fast arachnids can run has shown how leg anatomy and evolutionary history influence spiders’ running speed
author: James Woodford

title: The most detailed survey of the universe ever conducted starts now
description: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile is beginning its extraordinary survey of the southern sky, which will use the largest camera ever built to map the solar system, the galaxy and beyond
author: Leah Crane
